title: Avishay Tal | EECS at UC Berkeley
content: Avishay Tal | EECS at UC Berkeley Avishay Tal: Faculty Home Page Larger Photo Avishay Tal Associate Professor Contact Information 635 Soda Hall atal@berkeley.edu Links Personal Homepage Dissertations Research Support Rachel Zhang zhany21@berkeley.edu Biography Avishay Tal is a member of Berkeley's Theory Group. He received his Ph.D. in 2015 from the Weizmann Institute of Science under the guidance of Ran Raz. Prior to that, he completed a Master's degree at the Technion under the guidance of Amir Shpilka. He has held postdoctoral appointments at the Institute for Advanced Study (hosted by Avi Wigderson), Simons Institute (as part of the Lower Bounds in Computational Complexity program), and Stanford University (hosted by Omer Reingold). His research interests are in computational complexity, analysis of boolean functions, circuit complexity, formula complexity, query complexity, pseudorandomness, learning, combinatorics, quantum computing, and the connections between algorithms and lower bounds. Education 2015, PhD, Computer Science, Weizmann Institute of Science 2012, MS, Computer Science, The Technion, Haifa, Israel 2007, BA, Mathematics, The Technion, Haifa, Israel 2005, BS, Software Engineering, The Technion, Haifa, Israel Research Areas Theory (THY) Research Centers Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing (SITC) Selected Honors and Awards Sloan Research Fellow , 2022
